name: Cinnamon Rolls
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Very warm water (120 to 130 degrees)
1 cup Sifted powdered sugar (sift, then measure)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch 3/3/97
In a large bowl, combine 1 cup all-purpose flour, granulated sugar, undissolved yeast, dry milk powder, and salt. Stir in water, pear baby food, and whole-wheat flour. Stir in egg whites until the dough is soft and pliable.
On a lightly floured surface, knead the dough for about 4 to 6 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover; let rest on a floured surface for 10 minutes.
Using a rolling pin, shape the dough into an 18-by-12-inch rectangle. Brush honey over the dough, then evenly sprinkle with brown sugar, cinnamon, and raisins. Starting at one long end, roll up tightly and pinch the seam to seal.
Cut into 12 equal pieces and place cut sides up in a greased 13-by-9-inch baking pan. Cover; let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, about 30 to 45 minutes.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until done. Remove from the pan and let cool on a wire rack.
To prepare icing: In a small bowl, combine powdered sugar, milk, and vanilla until smooth. Drizzle over the rolls.
Yield: 12 rolls.
